summary: Crystal‑clear Betty soundboard captures a late‑71 powerhouse with a monster “The Other One” suite and a legendary Wharf Rat.
review: This recording is a Betty Cantor‑Jackson soundboard tape that sounds pristine – every instrument, especially Bill’s drums, shines through. The performance is one of the era’s finest: tight first set, an expansive second set highlighted by The Other One, Wharf Rat and Mr. Charlie, with the band already stretching toward the 1972 sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 enthusiastic reviews praise both the musical performance (standout jams, tight playing) and the audio quality (crisp, Betty soundboard). Minor criticisms are limited to tiny tape glitches, not enough to lower a near‑perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty board capture – crystal‑clear mix, superb instrument separation, drums exceptionally present. Reviewers note “crisp”, “clear”, “awesome sound”. Only occasional mention of tiny tape insertions that don’t affect overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as tight and solid; second set launches into long, exploratory jams (The Other One suite, Wharf Rat) that signal the band’s shift toward the 1972 sound. Overall pacing is praised for its energy and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals on Wharf Rat and Sugaree called “some of his best ever”; guitar tone described as perfect and weaving intricate leads.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass runs called “angular” and “rule the middle jam”, providing a strong foundation throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm work noted as tight, especially during interwoven leads with Pigpen on early songs like Truckin'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s playing highlighted as expressive, locked in with Pigpen; keyboards praised for beautiful fills and blend.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drums are singled out for clarity and presence – “crisp” and “stellar” in the mix.
